title: lachemilla involucrata
intro: Lachemilla involucrata, also known as rock pink or white rock rose, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lachemilla involucrata are thought to include wound healing, relief from fever and pain, and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Lachemilla involucrata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
